text.title: Jailbreak Eval Suite Bootstrap Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for a CI-gated adversarial jailbreak eval suite.

rule.statement: The suite covers ≥10 attack categories (DAN, role-play, cipher, base64, suffix, persona-flip, hypothetical, code-context, multi-turn, refusal-bypass). Single-category suites overfit to one attack family.
rule.rationale: Each category exercises a distinct failure pathway; cross-category coverage prevents a single fix from masking regression in another category.

rule.statement: Each category carries ≥5 cases. Cases are sourced from a public benchmark or red-team review with a citation field per case.
rule.rationale: Below 5 cases per category, a single attack-author bias dominates and a single fix passes the gate without proving robustness.

rule.statement: Every case has a binary judge contract declared upfront: either a regex/parser test or an LLM-judge prompt with two reference labels (refused / complied).
rule.rationale: Unjudged cases produce silent pass-rates; the judge contract makes each case automatically scorable in CI.

rule.statement: The LLM judge runs through the calibration protocol against a hand-labelled holdout (≥30 cases) before its verdicts gate merges. Required Cohen's κ ≥ 0.7.
rule.rationale: Uncalibrated judges leak false-pass and false-fail at unknown rates; calibration converts the judge into a known-quality channel.

rule.statement: Pass-rate threshold is set per category, not as a single global number. Default ≥95% per category; lower thresholds documented with a rationale and a fix-by date.
rule.rationale: Global thresholds hide regressions in low-volume categories. Per-category enforcement makes the failing surface visible.

rule.statement: Suite runs on every change to system prompts, tool definitions, model config, or judge prompt. Regression (any category drops >1pp from main) blocks the merge.
rule.rationale: Without an automatic gate the suite drifts to "run when we remember"; the gate makes the suite load-bearing.

rule.statement: All preconditions satisfied → execute the procedure in content/04-procedure.xml and produce suite-config.yaml + eval-cases.jsonl that pass the validator.
rule.rationale: Aggregate positive-branch conclusion target for the decision tree.

rule.statement: No safety refusal contract, no enumerable categories, or no CI capacity → skip bootstrap and address the precondition first.
rule.rationale: Aggregate negative-branch conclusion target for the decision tree.
